description: Drives the @sebastian-software/standards rollout: stamps .repometa.json#standards against the package's manifest.json#currentVersion and runs the mechanical sync. Variant A is in force — a human merges every standards: PR after the two external agent runs (apply-judgement + pre-review) have posted their commit/comment; this preset deliberately sets no automerge.

packageRules.description: standards drift PR: direct PR (no dashboard checkbox), a human merges, and the external two-pass agent is driven by labels + the .standards marker files. No automerge by design (Variant A) — do not add automerge 'for consistency' with the other org-managed deps. commitMessagePrefix 'chore: ' is the primary fix for the live PR-title bug (title rendered as 'standards v2' instead of 'chore: standards v2' for custom-datasource PRs in Renovate 43+); if it is ever not enough, the fallback ladder is prTitleStrict:false + custom prTitle, then extends :semanticCommitTypeAll(chore) with local override. addLabels carries only 'standards:needs-agent' — the second label 'standards:needs-review' is set later by the external Agent run 1 after it has worked through pending.json; setting it earlier would make Agent run 2 fire on a still-unprocessed PR. postUpgradeTasks lives inside this rule (not at top level) so the apply invocation only fires for the standards datasource — a top-level postUpgradeTask runs for every Renovate PR in the repo, which would invoke the standards CLI on completely unrelated dependency updates (e.g. github-actions bumps in non-standards-managed repos like sebastian-software/listwerk).
